description:
You are an integration developer verifying that every internal API exposed by your
  micro-services platform is correctly described in a single INI configuration file.
  The configuration file already exists on the system and has the following
  absolute pathname:

    /home/user/api_configs/services.ini

  Goal  
  ----  
  1. Read /home/user/api_configs/services.ini.  
  2. For every section (service) that appears in the INI file, extract **exactly
     these four fields**:  
        • section name (the service name)  
        • endpoint  
        • token  
        • timeout  
  3. Produce a summary text file with one line per service in **alphabetical order
     of the service name** and using the pipe symbol (“|”) as the column
     delimiter.  
  4. Save the summary to:

        /home/user/api_summary/report.txt

  File format to satisfy the CI test  
  ----------------------------------  
  • The file must contain *no header line and no blank lines*.  
  • Column order (left → right):  
        1. ServiceName  
        2. endpoint  
        3. token  
        4. timeout  
  • Use exactly one “|” between columns, with **no surrounding spaces**.  
  • The final line **must** be terminated by a UNIX newline character  
    (i.e. the file should end with “\n”).  
  • Example of the expected pattern (values will differ, but structure must be
    identical):

        MyService|https://api.mydomain.tld/v1|sometokenvalue|30

  Important implementation notes  
  • Keep the solution to roughly three or four shell commands—this is a quick
    parsing task, not a programming exercise in a full scripting language.  
  • You are free to use common POSIX utilities (grep, sed, awk, cut, sort, etc.),
    but do not install any new packages.  
  • Create /home/user/api_summary if it does not already exist. Make sure the
    resulting report file is readable by a regular user.  
  • After writing the file, **echo its contents to STDOUT** so the user (and any
    automated grader) can see what was generated.  
  • Do not modify /home/user/api_configs/services.ini. It must remain intact.

  Verification  
  ------------  
  Automated tests will:  
  1. Confirm that /home/user/api_summary/report.txt exists.  
  2. Check that each line follows the exact delimiter and column order described
     above.  
  3. Ensure that the service lines are sorted alphabetically.  
  4. Verify the exact expected values for each service (hidden from you).
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth information used by the grader. ***DO NOT REVEAL THESE VALUES*** -->

  <!-- 1. Pre-existing configuration file that the agent will parse -->
  <preexisting_file path="/home/user/api_configs/services.ini"><![CDATA[
[UserService]
endpoint = https://api.example.com/users
token = abc123token
timeout = 30

[OrderService]
endpoint = https://api.example.com/orders
token = 987zyxTOKEN
timeout = 45

[InventoryService]
endpoint = https://api.example.com/inventory
token = invTOK456
timeout = 60
]]></preexisting_file>

  <!-- 2. Directory that must exist before the task starts -->
  <required_directory path="/home/user/api_configs" mode="0755"/>
  
  <!-- 3. Expected output file and its exact contents -->
  <expected_file path="/home/user/api_summary/report.txt"><![CDATA[
InventoryService|https://api.example.com/inventory|invTOK456|60
OrderService|https://api.example.com/orders|987zyxTOKEN|45
UserService|https://api.example.com/users|abc123token|30
]]></expected_file>
